hypothesis: If an LLM-based agent framework that iteratively retrieves domain-relevant information through specialized tools (memory, periodic table, knowledge base) and incorporates property evaluation feedback is used for inorganic materials design, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies while maintaining compositional validity, uniqueness, and novelty compared to tool-free LLM variants and non-iterative generative diffusion models, because the structured retrieval and feedback loop enables the agent to systematically refine compositional proposals using accumulated chemical knowledge and prior experimental outcomes.
